instructions.instruction: Use Bun-native file I/O APIs (Bun.file(), Bun.write()) instead of Node.js fs module calls (readFileSync, writeFileSync, fs.promises.*) in Bun-managed projects.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing or reviewing any file read/write operation in a Bun project
instructions.why_given: Mixed APIs create inconsistent behavior and bypass Bun performance advantages like lazy file loading.

instructions.instruction: Use bun install for dependency management in Bun repositories. Never run npm install or yarn install.
instructions.relevant_when: Installing, adding, or updating dependencies in any project using Bun
instructions.why_given: Mixed package managers cause lockfile drift and nondeterministic installs across environments.

instructions.instruction: Use prepared statements (db.prepare(...).all(param)) for all SQL queries where any input is user-controlled. Never interpolate untrusted values into query strings.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing or reviewing bun:sqlite database queries
instructions.why_given: Direct string interpolation enables SQL injection; prepared statements with bound parameters prevent it.

instructions.instruction: Never pass user-provided input as the command string to Bun.spawn(['sh', '-c', userInput]). Use Bun.file() or pass fixed argument arrays to Bun.spawn().
instructions.relevant_when: Writing shell invocations or subprocess code in Bun
instructions.why_given: Passing user input to sh -c enables command injection; using direct API calls or fixed argument arrays prevents it.

instructions.instruction: After any dependency change, run bun test to isolate regressions before proceeding. If bun install fails, check bun.lock or bun.lockb for merge conflicts.
instructions.relevant_when: Modifying package.json dependencies or workspace configuration
instructions.why_given: Dependency changes can silently break test suites; verifying early prevents downstream debugging.

instructions.instruction: For SQL validation, run: rg -n 'query\(|prepare\(' src and confirm that all user-controlled query paths use db.prepare(). If raw query() calls with interpolated values exist, refactor them before merging.
instructions.relevant_when: Reviewing a codebase for SQL safety before a release or PR merge
instructions.why_given: SQL injection through raw query() calls is a high-severity vulnerability that grep-level review can catch.
